articles.title: So you’ve heard these AI terms and nodded along; let’s fix that
articles.description: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

articles.title: Fintech startup Parker files for bankruptcy
articles.description: Parker, a well-funded startup offering corporate credit cards and banking services, has filed for bankruptcy and is widely reported to have shut down.

articles.title: GM agrees to pay $12.75M in California driver privacy settlement
articles.description: General Motors has reached a privacy-related settlement with a group of law enforcement agencies led by California Attorney General Rob Bonta.

articles.title: The Instax Wide 400 builds on instant photography’s simplicity and stretches it, literally
articles.description: In an AI and digital world, analog instant film and retro-style cameras continue to remain popular, fueled by a mix of both nostalgia and novelty.

articles.title: Nvidia has already committed $40B to equity AI deals this year
articles.description: Nvidia continues to be a big investor in the AI ecosystem.

articles.title: Laid-off Oracle workers tried to negotiate better severance. Oracle said no.
articles.description: Some found out they didn't qualify for WARN Act protections like two-months notice because the company had classified them as remote workers.

articles.title: San Francisco’s housing market has lost its mind
articles.description: The invisible force behind all of this is no mystery to anyone paying attention to the city's tech economy. San Francisco is home to some of the most valuable private companies in the world, and their employees have been quietly accumulating — and, increasingly, cashing out — fortunes.

articles.title: Prime Video follows Netflix and Disney by adding a TikTok-like ‘Clips’ feed in its app
articles.description: The Clips feed aims to enable discovery by offering users a scrollable feed with short snippets of shows and movies.

articles.title: Intel’s comeback story is even wilder than it seems
articles.description: Intel's stock has risen a stunning 490% over the past year, a bet by Wall Street that may be running well ahead of the company's actual turnaround.

articles.title: Cloudflare says AI made 1,100 jobs obsolete, even as revenue hit a record high
articles.description: Cloudflare announced its first large-scale layoff. CEO Matthew Prince says because of AI efficiency gains, the company doesn't need as many support roles.

articles.title: Porsche shutters e-bike, battery, software subsidiaries as part of company overhaul
articles.description: More than 500 people will be affected by the closures. “We must refocus on our core business,” Porsche CEO and executive chair Michael Leiters said in a statement. "This is the indispensable foundation for a successful strategic realignment. This forces us to make painful cuts — including our subsidiaries."

articles.title: Mother Ventures is looking at moms as the ‘economic engine’
articles.description: The VC firm, which focuses on mothers as consumers, raised a $10 million debut fund.

articles.title: Uber partner Avride is under investigation for self-driving crashes
articles.description: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration has opened an investigation into Avride after identifying more than a dozen crashes and one minor injury.

articles.title: Poland says hackers breached water treatment plants, and the US is facing the same threat
articles.description: A report by Poland’s top intelligence agency accused Russia of sabotage and hacking activities against the country’s military and civilian infrastructure.

articles.title: US defense contractor who sold hacking tools to Russian broker ordered to pay $10M to former employers
articles.description: Former cybersecurity executive Peter Williams stole several surveillance and hacking tools and sold them for $1.3 million to a Russian broker that works with Putin’s government.

articles.title: Pentagon releases UFO files on new website
articles.description: The initial set of files housed on the site will include those containing unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAP) videos, photos, and original source documents from across the entire U.S. government. The materials have been reviewed for security purposes, but many have "not yet been analyzed for resolution of any anomalies," the Department's statement read.

articles.title: Truecaller slashes 70 jobs amid declining ad sales
articles.description: Truecaller's ad revenue declined by 44%, leading to the layoffs.

articles.title: Live only at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026: Why most founders are already behind on raising a Series A in 2027
articles.description: Learn what it takes to build a Series A in 2027 from top VCs at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, taking place at San Francisco’s Moscone West on October 13-15. Register today to hear this session live on the Builders Stage.

articles.title: Last 24 hours to get 50% off a second pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
articles.description: Last day to buy one pass and get a second one at 50% off to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Bring a partner, co-founder, or colleague at half off. Register now.

articles.title: Lime, the Uber-backed micromobility company, files for IPO
articles.description: After years of hints and preparation, Lime plans to enter the public markets. The company intends to list on ​Nasdaq under the ticker symbol "LIME." Lime did not share terms of the offering.

articles.description: These connected companions could disrupt everything from make-believe to bedtime stories. No wonder some lawmakers want them banned.

articles.description: The plan isn't final and could change, but his ouster would be no surprise.

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **AI行业投资与影响**：英伟达今年已承诺向AI股权交易投入400亿美元，同时Cloudflare表示AI导致1100个岗位被淘汰，尽管公司收入创历史新高。
2. **企业动态与破产**：金融科技初创公司Parker申请破产；保时捷因公司重组关闭了电动自行车、电池和软件子公司；英特尔复兴之路比表面更复杂。
3. **隐私与安全**：通用汽车因加州驾驶员隐私问题支付1275万美元和解金；波兰称黑客入侵水处理厂，美国面临同样威胁；美国国防承包商因向俄罗斯中介出售黑客工具被判赔偿前雇主1000万美元。
4. **产品与市场变化**：Prime Video效仿Netflix和迪士尼，在应用中新增类似TikTok的“片段”功能；富士Instax Wide 400相机在保持拍立得简洁性的同时扩展了画幅；旧金山房地产市场出现异常波动。
5. **劳工与法律纠纷**：被裁员的甲骨文员工试图协商更优遣散费遭拒绝；Uber合作伙伴Avride因自动驾驶事故正接受调查。

今日最值得关注的发展：英伟达以400亿美元AI投资领跑行业，而AI技术同时加速了就业替代，凸显技术变革的双刃剑效应。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **医疗AI与隐私保护**：OncoAgent提出双层多智能体框架，用于隐私保护的肿瘤临床决策支持（Hugging Face）；OpenAI推出GPT-5.5及网络安全专用版本，强化可信访问与网络防御能力。
2. **模型与架构创新**：EMO通过预训练混合专家模型实现新兴模块化能力；vLLM从V0到V1版本强调强化学习中“修正先于纠错”的设计理念。
3. **AI应用与工具扩展**：Google发布AI辅助小企业广告创意案例、搜索园艺技巧、与XPRIZE合作推出350万美元未来视觉电影竞赛；OpenAI在API中新增语音智能模型、Webhooks支持长时任务，并测试ChatGPT广告及“可信联系人”功能。
4. **开放性与评测优化**：Hugging Face在ASR语音识别排行榜中加入“Benchmaxxer”反作弊机制，提升评测公平性。
5. **安全与可靠性**：OpenAI分享Codex安全运行经验，强调在强化学习与部署中确保模型行为的正确性与一致性。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：多家机构在医疗、语音、网络安全等垂直领域推出隐私保护与可信访问的AI框架，同时通过反作弊机制和模型安全实践，推动AI从能力突破向可信落地迈进。

cctv.summary:
今日新闻联播动态摘要

1. **中国科技自主创新加速发展**：包括中国推出首台双核原子量子计算机“汉原二号”、AI公司DeepSeek以450亿美元估值寻求融资，以及中国AI实验室内部进展的报道，凸显中国在量子计算和人工智能领域的自主研发能力。

2. **中美科技竞争与博弈**：多篇报道聚焦中美在AI领域的竞争态势，如美国推动AI护栏以防止危机升级、中国供应链从手机向人形机器人转型，以及美国对中国科技发展的关注与限制。

3. **国际事件与地缘政治**：东京审判揭露靖国神社的错误叙事、伊朗战争引发的全球能源危机凸显中国清洁技术优势，以及美国国内枪击事件反映社会矛盾，显示中国在国际事务中的立场与影响力。

4. **中国青年与经济发展**：报道中国年轻人“逃离内卷”的社会现象，以及从白银、丝绸到国家关系的明清历史分析，展现中国社会结构变迁与历史纵深。

5. **美国对华指控与事实澄清**：针对美国媒体所谓“中国大规模拘留与监控”的指控，相关视频报道予以反驳，强调中国法治与人权保障的实际情况。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：中国在量子计算与AI领域的连续突破，以及中美围绕AI技术主导权的博弈持续升级，凸显中国正加速从“应用创新”向“核心技术自主”转型。
